summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on zoning case ZC 2023-003, a request to rezone two lots on Valley Lane from B1 (business) to R6 (residential) for construction of two duplexes (four total units) near the intersection of Goshen Road and State Route 28. Staff recommended approval based on the existing residential character of Valley Lane and the growth management plan. The commission discussed concerns about setting a precedent for high-density R6 zoning and potential future implications for adjacent properties. The rezoning was approved 3-1, and the regular meeting addressed old business including a pending zoning case awaiting an impact study.
decisions: Approved ZC 2023-003 rezoning from B1 to R6 with staff recommendations (3-1 vote: Currie-Yes, Dinwiddie-Yes, Lewis-No, Otkey-Yes)
action_items: Continue monitoring ZC 2023-001 pending impact study completion
Liz Fields / McBride Dale Clary to discuss potential R4 alternative zoning with applicant if needed
Applicant (Mr. Taylor) to proceed with meeting R6 code requirements for proposed duplexes
key_amounts: 4 total residential units proposed (two duplexes) on two lots
Applicant's two lots combined are approximately 1 acre
Estimated 1-year construction timeline for the proposed homes
notable_topics: Rezoning of two lots on Valley Lane from B1 to R6 for duplex construction
Concerns about setting a precedent for high-density R6 zoning in the township
History of R6 zoning issues on Snyder Road referenced as cautionary example
Consistency with Township comprehensive/growth management plan recommending 2-4 residential units
Existing residential character of Valley Lane despite B1 zoning designation
Potential future lot combination and higher density development concerns
attendees: Liz Fields (Zoning and Planning Consultant, McBride Dale Clary)
notes: The transcript contains significant portions of garbled/corrupted text, likely due to poor audio quality or transcription errors. The Clermont County Regional Planning Commission apparently reviewed the application and recommended R4 zoning rather than R6, partly due to a late submission and incomplete application details. There was notable concern from commission members about the only other R6-zoned property in Goshen Township being on Snyder Road, which apparently led to past legal issues. Mr. Lewis cast the sole dissenting vote. The next meeting was scheduled for May 2nd at 6:30 PM.
